title: Brake system
abstract: A brake system may include an actuating device, in particular a brake pedal; a first piston-cylinder unit having two pistons subjecting the brake circuits to a pressure medium via a valve device, wherein one of the pistons can be actuated by the actuation device; a second piston-cylinder unit having an electric motor drive, a transmission at least one piston to supply at least one of the brake circuits with a pressure medium via a valve device; and a motor pump unit with a valve device to supply the brake circuits with a pressure medium. The brake system may also include a hydraulic travel simulator with a pressure or working chamber which is connected to the first piston-cylinder unit.
claims: 1. A system comprising: a two-box brake system, a vehicle having axles, wheel brakes being assigned to at least one of the axles, and an actuating device in the form of a brake pedal for controlling brake pressure supplied to the wheel brakes; wherein the two-box brake system comprises an electric brake booster as a part of a first box and an electronic stability control (ESP) unit as a part of a second box, the electric brake booster being connected to the ESP unit via two hydraulic lines, the first hydraulic line being part of a first hydraulic brake circuit and the second hydraulic line being part of a second hydraulic brake circuit, wherein both the electric brake booster and the ESP unit are able to supply brake pressure to the wheel brakes via the brake circuits, wherein the wheel brakes are connected to the brake circuits, wherein each of the wheel brakes comprises a brake pad, a brake disc, and a rollback seal which is deformed when brake pressure is built up in the respective wheel brake to press the brake pad against the brake disk, wherein the actuating device is connected to the brake pads of the wheel brakes via the hydraulic brake circuits, wherein each rollback seal is dimensioned such that a deformation energy stored therein is sufficiently high to lift the brake pad from the brake disc for clearance as soon as there is no pressure supplied to the respective wheel brake by the actuating device and/or the electric booster and/or the ESP unit any more, wherein the clearance between the brake pad and the brake disc allows a decoupling of the actuating device from the respective wheel brake, wherein in case pressure is supplied to the respective wheel brake, via a piston of the electric brake booster, both the electric brake booster and the ESP unit are able to overcome the clearance, wherein when pressure is supplied to the respective wheel brake via the piston of the electric brake booster, the pressure is supplied using position control of the piston of the electric brake booster to adjust the pressure supplied via the piston of the electric brake booster.
